title: hieracium macilentum subsp. staui
intro: Hieracium macilentum subsp. staui, also known as the Staunton's hawkweed or Staunton's yellow hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Wales and other parts of the UK. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Hieracium macilentum subsp. staui include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. Herbal preparations of Hieracium macilentum subsp. staui typically involve drying the plant's leaves and flowers to make a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
